title: GDPR Consent Mechanism Audit (Lawful Basis Alignment)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
GDPR Art.21 Right to object — UC audits the consent mechanism against lawful-basis alignment, covering the opt-out / objection propagation that Art.21 requires.
Derived from GDPR@2016/679 Art.21 (parent assurance='partial'). UK GDPR preserves parent clause numbering; inherited assurance degraded one step.

app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809)
spl:
(index=web sourcetype="js_tag") earliest=-24h
| eval before_consent=if(consent_state="unknown" AND match(tag_category,"(?i)marketing|analytics"),1,0)
| stats sum(before_consent) as early_nonessential_tags by site_id tag_vendor
| where early_nonessential_tags > 100
| lookup gdpr_consent_policy.csv site_id OUTPUT required_banner_version
| join site_id [
    search index=consent earliest=-24h
    | stats latest(banner_version) as live_banner by site_id
]
| where live_banner != required_banner_version
| table site_id, tag_vendor, early_nonessential_tags, live_banner, required_banner_version
value: By design, consent collection must be granular and verifiable. This use case monitors CMP and web telemetry for consent banner versions, non-essential cookies fired before consent, and mismatches between claimed purposes in the CMP and tags observed in the browser stream.
implementation: (1) Instrument sites to emit tag and consent state events; (2) define required banner versions per site in policy lookup; (3) alert marketing and privacy when non-essential tags load pre-consent; (4) block releases that downgrade banner version without approval; (5) align with UC-22.1.5/UC-22.1.16 for withdrawal enforcement.
visualization: Table (sites with violations), Single value (sites off-policy), Heatmap (violations by region), Bar chart (tag vendors).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
